meta_title: For Founders: analytics setup to Solve tutorial hell (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome tutorial hell. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Analytics Setup to Solve Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Stop drowning in tutorials. Learn how to implement analytics for your MVP using Antigravity and Claude Code to validate your SaaS and scale faster.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell ends when you stop consuming and start measuring. By integrating lightweight analytics into your MVP, you force yourself to focus on user behavior rather than syntax. Use Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for automated implementation. This shifts your focus from coding theory to revenue-generating user insights.
content.intro:
You are caught in the infinite loop of 'one more video,' watching your runway burn while your product remains a ghost in a terminal. Every tutorial promises the moon, but delivers nothing that ships. You aren't building a product; you are building a graveyard of abandoned repositories.

The antidote isn't a better tutorial—it's radical accountability through data. By embedding analytics into your MVP today, you stop guessing what your users want and start building what they pay for. This isn't just about code; it's about business survival.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metric
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the one user action that correlates with revenue. If you can't measure it, you shouldn't be building it.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a SaaS Product Manager. Analyze my business model and suggest 3 key events I should track in my MVP to validate product-market fit.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review my business logic and define the 3 most critical conversion events for my MVP tracking implementation.'

steps.title: Architect the Analytics Layer
steps.description: Use Antigravity to scaffold a clean, decoupled analytics provider pattern so you can switch tools without refactoring your entire codebase.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, reusable TypeScript analytics interface and a provider implementation for PostHog that handles batching and error suppression.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create analytics/analytics.ts and integrate the generated provider pattern into the root layout.'

steps.title: Automate Event Instrumentation
steps.description: Don't manually track every button. Use Claude Code to inject tracking hooks into your core user journey components automatically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a HOC or Hook that wraps my primary CTAs to automatically trigger tracking events on click.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Find all primary CTA buttons in components/ and wrap them with the useTracking hook.'

steps.title: Implement User Identification
steps.description: Analytics are useless without context. Link events to specific user IDs to understand the journey from visitor to paying customer.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a logic block to sync Auth0 or Clerk user IDs with my analytics provider upon successful session initialization.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Add user identification logic to the session provider to ensure all events are tagged with the current user ID.'

steps.title: Automated Testing for Tracking
steps.description: Never ship broken data. Use Claude Code to write a test suite that ensures events fire when they are supposed to.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Playwright test script that simulates a user sign-up flow and asserts that the 'signup_completed' event is fired.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create tests/analytics.spec.ts and verify the signup flow event tracking with Playwright.'

pro_tips: Track 'negative' events like modal closes or error states; they often reveal more about friction than successful conversions.
Never send PII (Personally Identifiable Information) to your analytics provider; use hashed IDs to remain GDPR/CCPA compliant.
Implement an 'Analytics-First' flag in your CI/CD pipeline to block deployments if core tracking events are missing.
Don't track everything. Tracking too much leads to 'data noise' that paralyzes decision-making.
Use session recording in tandem with event tracking to visually confirm why a user dropped off at a specific step.
content.next_steps: Deploy your MVP to a staging environment, run your tracking tests, and launch to your first 10 users to gather real data.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in a vacuum. Your product needs data to survive, and you need a community to thrive. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
